summary: The day is dominated by heavy-ion theory and phenomenology rather than new experimental data. The strongest item links nonlinear collective flow in ultra-central U+U collisions to the sign of intrinsic hexadecapole deformation, while CGC NLO wave-function work contributes small-x theoretical infrastructure. Fluctuation and dipolar-flow papers offer useful Beam Energy Scan and transport-oriented baselines, and a collider-specific diffusion surrogate provides the clearest AI/ML contribution with detector-level validation.
strongest_paper: 2607.18752: nonlinear collective flow as an experimentally accessible probe of the sign of beta4 in U+U collisions.

papers.title: Nonlinear collective flow reveals the breakdown of quadrupole--hexadecapole scaling in heavy ion collisions
papers.authors_short: Hadi Mehrabpour, Zahra Sheibani, Li Yan, Chunjian Zhang, Abolfazl Mirjalili
papers.abstract: Using event-by-event viscous hydrodynamic simulations of ultra-central U-238+U-238 collisions at sqrt(sNN)=193 GeV, the paper studies whether higher-order collective flow can isolate the contribution of intrinsic hexadecapole deformation and test beta2-beta4 correlations. It argues that beta4 sign information survives QGP evolution and is enhanced through nonlinear hydrodynamic response, especially in xi_6,222.

papers.title: Soft Gluon Wave Function and Evolution Operator in the CGC at Next-to-Leading Order
papers.abstract: Constructs the soft-gluon light-cone wave function and unitary evolution operator Omega through order g^2 in pure Yang-Mills theory within the CGC framework, using unitarity and light-cone perturbation theory to fix normal-ordered coefficients.

papers.title: Transport phenomena and observables associated with viscous properties of an anisotropic hot QCD medium at finite baryon asymmetry
papers.abstract: Computes viscous transport coefficients and related observables for weakly anisotropic hot QCD matter at finite baryon asymmetry using a quasiparticle model and Boltzmann transport equation in relaxation-time approximation.

papers.title: Sensitivity of p_T Fluctuations to the QCD Equation of State
papers.authors_short: Rutik Manikandhan, Rohit Gupta, Rene Bellwied
papers.abstract: Develops a theoretical baseline for dynamical transverse-momentum correlations C_pT from sqrt(sNN)=3 to 200 GeV using lattice-QCD-informed equation-of-state inputs at finite baryochemical potential, with emphasis on Beam Energy Scan interpretation.

papers.title: Rapidity-even Dipolar Flow in Relativistic Heavy-Ion Collisions
papers.abstract: Studies rapidity-even directed flow and multiparticle correlations in Au+Au collisions at 200 GeV with AMPT and HIJING, using eta-dependent weighting to suppress global momentum conservation contributions.

papers.title: Ultra-peripheral Collisions
papers.authors_short: Jesus Guillermo Contreras, Spencer Robert Klein
papers.abstract: Review of photon-induced physics in ultra-peripheral collisions of heavy ions and protons, including low-x nuclear structure, high-energy photon probes, and interferometric quantum-mechanics applications.

papers.title: D(e+e-)ffusion: Capturing the Beam-Beam Physics of e+e- Collisions with Diffusion Models
papers.authors_short: Antonio Chahine, Mariarosaria D'Alfonso, Jan Eysermans, Emmett Forrestel, et al.
papers.abstract: Introduces a permutation-equivariant denoising diffusion surrogate for incoherent-pair beam backgrounds at future e+e- colliders. The paper validates generated events with physics distributions, Geant4 propagation through a CLD vertex-detector setup, and a transformer two-sample classifier, reporting nearly four orders of magnitude generation speedup.
